status: exact positive-boundary attainment and arbitrary-multiplicity energy lemma
proof_boundary: This artifact composes published positive-time strip and high-zero theorems with compactness to prove that Lambda>0 would force a finite real multiple zero of H_Lambda. It derives the exact arbitrary-multiplicity Hermite cluster energy blow-up and a positive-time simplicity equivalence. It does not prove positive-time simplicity, endpoint energy integrability, RH, or Lambda<=0.

published_strip.statement: Since H_0 has no zero with |Im z|>1, every zero of H_t for 0<t<=1/2 satisfies |Im z|<=sqrt(1-2t)<=1.
published_strip.scope: Absolute vertical compactness for every positive time up to 1/2.

published_high_zero.statement: There are absolute C,c>0 such that, uniformly for 0<t<=1/2, if x>=exp(C/t) and H_t(x+iy)=0 then y=0; each sufficiently high reference disk contains exactly one zero, which is real.

exact.positive_boundary_compactness: Assume Lambda>0 and take t_n=Lambda-1/n with n large, so Lambda/2<=t_n<Lambda. Every H_(t_n) has a nonreal zero z_n. With X_Lambda=exp(2*C/Lambda), strip contraction and Theorem 1.5 give |Re z_n|<X_Lambda and |Im z_n|<=1.
exact.local_uniform_convergence: The Fourier integral and super-exponential decay imply H_t -> H_Lambda locally uniformly as t->Lambda.
exact.multiplicity_argument: A subsequence z_n converges to z_*. Since H_Lambda has only real zeros, z_* is real. The distinct zeros z_n and conjugate(z_n) both converge to z_*; Rouche zero counting forces mult_(z_*) H_Lambda>=2.
exact.attainment_theorem: If Lambda>0, H_Lambda has a finite real multiple zero c with |c|<=X_Lambda=exp(2*C/Lambda). Thus a positive Newman boundary cannot be realized solely by zero collisions escaping to infinity.
exact.positive_time_simplicity_equivalence: Using simplicity for every t>Lambda and the published bound Lambda<=1/5, Lambda<=0 if and only if H_t has only simple zeros for every 0<t<=1/5.

published_hermite_split.statement: At a multiplicity-m zero c at time t_*, the nearby zeros for tau=t-t_*>0 are c+sqrt(2*tau)*lambda_a+O(tau), where lambda_a are the simple real roots of the probabilists' Hermite He_m.

cluster_energy.renormalized_cluster: The Rodgers-Tao linear reference corrections are O(1)+O(sqrt(tau)), so the ordered renormalized cluster has the same leading term.
cluster_energy.integral: integral_0^epsilon E_cluster(tau) dtau=+infinity for every m>=2

exact.conditional_global_exclusion: Under Lambda>0 the multiple cluster is finite. Therefore any nonnegative finite truncation containing it, if proved time-integrable on (Lambda,Lambda+epsilon), would contradict the universal cluster blow-up and force Lambda<=0.
exact.open_handoff: Prove either Xi positive-time simplicity for every 0<t<=1/5, or an Xi-specific finite-truncation energy estimate that remains integrable down to every hypothetical positive boundary. The published Rodgers-Tao estimate starts strictly after a negative boundary and does not supply this endpoint control.

rows.claim: Positive-time Xi zeros remain in one absolute horizontal strip.
rows.formula: Since H_0 has no zero with |Im z|>1, every zero of H_t for 0<t<=1/2 satisfies |Im z|<=sqrt(1-2t)<=1.
rows.proof_boundary: Theorem 3.2 composed with the critical-strip bound at t=0.

diagnostics.statement: Since H_0 has no zero with |Im z|>1, every zero of H_t for 0<t<=1/2 satisfies |Im z|<=sqrt(1-2t)<=1.
diagnostics.scope: Absolute vertical compactness for every positive time up to 1/2.

rows.claim: All sufficiently high positive-time zeros are uniformly real and uniquely localized.
rows.formula: There are absolute C,c>0 such that, uniformly for 0<t<=1/2, if x>=exp(C/t) and H_t(x+iy)=0 then y=0; each sufficiently high reference disk contains exactly one zero, which is real.
rows.proof_boundary: Uniform for 0<t<=1/2 with an exp(C/t) threshold.

diagnostics.statement: There are absolute C,c>0 such that, uniformly for 0<t<=1/2, if x>=exp(C/t) and H_t(x+iy)=0 then y=0; each sufficiently high reference disk contains exactly one zero, which is real.

rows.claim: Under Lambda>0, nonreal zeros immediately below Lambda lie in one fixed compact rectangle.
rows.formula: Assume Lambda>0 and take t_n=Lambda-1/n with n large, so Lambda/2<=t_n<Lambda. Every H_(t_n) has a nonreal zero z_n. With X_Lambda=exp(2*C/Lambda), strip contraction and Theorem 1.5 give |Re z_n|<X_Lambda and |Im z_n|<=1.
rows.proof_boundary: Conditional contradiction regime Lambda>0; C is the published absolute constant.

rows.claim: Compact conjugate zero pairs below the boundary converge to a multiple real boundary zero.
rows.formula: The Fourier integral and super-exponential decay imply H_t -> H_Lambda locally uniformly as t->Lambda. A subsequence z_n converges to z_*. Since H_Lambda has only real zeros, z_* is real. The distinct zeros z_n and conjugate(z_n) both converge to z_*; Rouche zero counting forces mult_(z_*) H_Lambda>=2.
rows.proof_boundary: Local uniform convergence and Rouche zero counting.

rows.claim: A hypothetical positive Newman boundary is attained by a finite real multiple zero.
rows.formula: If Lambda>0, H_Lambda has a finite real multiple zero c with |c|<=X_Lambda=exp(2*C/Lambda). Thus a positive Newman boundary cannot be realized solely by zero collisions escaping to infinity.
rows.proof_boundary: Composition of the two published positive-time theorems and compactness.

rows.claim: The Newman upper direction is equivalent to simplicity on the published reduced positive-time window.
rows.formula: Using simplicity for every t>Lambda and the published bound Lambda<=1/5, Lambda<=0 if and only if H_t has only simple zeros for every 0<t<=1/5.
rows.proof_boundary: Still an all-zero continuum theorem on 0<t<=1/5; no simplicity proof is supplied here.

rows.claim: Every multiplicity-m boundary zero opens according to the universal Hermite cluster.
rows.formula: At a multiplicity-m zero c at time t_*, the nearby zeros for tau=t-t_*>0 are c+sqrt(2*tau)*lambda_a+O(tau), where lambda_a are the simple real roots of the probabilists' Hermite He_m.
rows.proof_boundary: Proposition 3.1(ii) of the cited Polymath paper.

diagnostics.statement: At a multiplicity-m zero c at time t_*, the nearby zeros for tau=t-t_*>0 are c+sqrt(2*tau)*lambda_a+O(tau), where lambda_a are the simple real roots of the probabilists' Hermite He_m.

rows.claim: The Hermite cluster has an exact total reciprocal-square stiffness.
rows.proof_boundary: Algebraic consequence of the probabilists' Hermite ODE.

rows.claim: Every finite multiple boundary cluster has logarithmically nonintegrable renormalized energy.
rows.formula: sum_(a!=b) 1/(x_a-x_b)^2=m*(m-1)/(8*tau)+O(tau^(-1/2)); integral_0^epsilon E_cluster(tau) dtau=+infinity for every m>=2
rows.proof_boundary: Unweighted ordered cluster, or any truncation with positive weights on one pair.

rows.claim: The collision route reduces to positive-time simplicity or finite boundary-energy integrability.
rows.formula: Prove either Xi positive-time simplicity for every 0<t<=1/5, or an Xi-specific finite-truncation energy estimate that remains integrable down to every hypothetical positive boundary. The published Rodgers-Tao estimate starts strictly after a negative boundary and does not supply this endpoint control.
rows.proof_boundary: Open Xi-specific endpoint theorem; not RH and not Lambda<=0.
